column61: The customer was facing a poor voice-related problem. When others tried to call him, it often said that his number was switched off, although his number was active, and he could sometimes receive calls. The CM checked the number and found everything fine, then raised a complaint proactively without allowing the customer to express any disagreement or provide further details, such as facing signal-related issues (e.g., network bar fluctuating frequently). Furthermore, the CM raised a close complaint and informed the customer that the issue was being forwarded and the concerned team would work on it and notify him via call or SMS. This was incorrect, as a close complaint does not trigger any action from the concerned team, nor will they notify the customer. By failing to capture the customer’s actual issue and the CM provided misleading information, which negatively impacted the customer’s trust and service experience.

column64: The customer visited to inquire why he was charged 60 Taka. The CM explained that the charge was due to the Adda Café service. The customer clarified that he had only activated the Adda Café service and had not purchased any separate minute package, questioning why he was charged 60 Taka without prior notification. The CM then incorrectly explained that the activation charge was 43.17 Taka and that an additional cost was added for 630 minutes, totaling 60 Taka. This was wrong. In reality, the Adda Café 30-day package costs 43.17 Taka without VAT, SD, and SC; with VAT, SD, and SC included, the total charge is 60 Taka. By purchasing the 30-day Adda Café service for 60 Taka, the customer receives both the service and the bundled minutes. Additionally, the CM incorrectly stated that the Adda Café service was activated through the MyGP App also , which was wrong. The CM failed to explain the package details correctly, resulting in customer confusion

CE : VAS, The customer visited to inquire why he was charged 60 Taka. The CM explained that the charge was due to the Adda Café service. The customer clarified that he had only activated the Adda Café service and had not purchased any separate minute package, questioning why he was charged 60 Taka without prior notification. The CM then incorrectly explained that the activation charge was 43.17 Taka and that an additional cost was added for 630 minutes, totaling 60 Taka. This was wrong. In reality, the Adda Café 30-day package costs 43.17 Taka without VAT, SD, and SC; with VAT, SD, and SC included, the total charge is 60 Taka. By purchasing the 30-day Adda Café service for 60 Taka, the customer receives both the service and the bundled minutes. Additionally, the CM incorrectly stated that the Adda Café service was activated through the MyGP App also , which was wrong. The CM failed to explain the package details correctly, resulting in customer confusion
